# Independent Enrollment Study

**Initiative:** [INITIATIVE-005](../../docs/initiative/Active/(INITIATIVE-005)-Independent-Enrollment-Study/(INITIATIVE-005)-Independent-Enrollment-Study.md)
**Status:** In Progress

---

Independent analysis of South Portland's elementary enrollment trends, patterns, and projections. The district's reconfiguration proposals rest on enrollment assumptions that have not been independently verified.

## Contents

### Briefings

The [briefings/](briefings/) folder contains persona-specific briefings covering the full budget picture — staffing cuts, school closure impact, enrollment trends, and fiscal analysis. Each of the 15 personas receives findings framed for their specific concerns and information needs.

| Briefing | Audience |
|----------|----------|
| [General Budget Briefing](briefings/general-budget-briefing.md) | Anyone -- covers the full budget picture |
| [General Upcoming Briefing](briefings/general-upcoming-briefing.md) | Anyone -- what to watch at the next budget meeting |
| Persona briefs (001-015) | See individual files in [briefings/](briefings/) |

### Data

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [nces-enrollment-by-grade.csv](nces-enrollment-by-grade.csv) | NCES Common Core of Data enrollment by grade |
| [nces-enrollment-by-grade.json](nces-enrollment-by-grade.json) | Same data in JSON format |

## Scope

This initiative covers:
- Enrollment data acquisition from NCES, Maine DOE, and district sources
- Enrollment gap analysis — what the district claims vs. what the data shows
- Cohort survival modeling and 5-year projections
- Per-persona briefs translating enrollment findings into stakeholder-relevant analysis

## Source Data

- **NCES Common Core of Data** — Federal enrollment counts by grade, school, and year
- **Maine DOE enrollment reports** — State-level enrollment data
- **District budget documents** — Enrollment figures cited in budget presentations
- **School board meeting transcripts** — Enrollment claims and projections discussed publicly

## Disclaimer

This is an independent analysis using publicly available data. It is not affiliated with or endorsed by the South Portland School Department.
